basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Al-Jāthiya — “The Kneeling” — is a Meccan sūra that leads the reader from the signs of creation to the great reckoning. It opens upon the proofs scattered through the heavens, in man's own constitution and in the alternation of the days, meant “for a people of certainty.” Qushayrī teaches that the witnesses of Lordship shine forth, and that whoever sobers from the drunkenness of heedlessness unfailingly attains the realities of union. The sūra then denounces the drama of the soul that takes its own desire as its god, whom God leads astray knowingly, sealing his hearing and his heart. Qushayrī warns that the one who does not strip himself of his whim and is trained by no exemplary master drifts into every pit, his bridle in the hand of his passion. It culminates on the striking vision of the Day when one will see “every community kneeling,” bowed upon its knees, summoned to its Book. To the righteous believers the Lord opens the entrance into His mercy — the manifest triumph — while the sūra is sealed upon praise and the Grandeur of the Lord of the worlds.
themes: desire set up as an idol

versets.ar: حم
versets.tr: ḥā mīm
versets.trad: Ḥā. Mīm.
versets.commentaire: The two isolated letters open the sūra upon the threshold of mystery. Qushayrī sees in these detached letters at once a veil and a sign: God addresses to His servant what He alone fully knows, and the attentive soul senses in them the majesty of the Word before grasping its meaning.

versets.ar: تَنْزِيلُ الْكِتَابِ مِنَ اللَّهِ الْعَزِيزِ الْحَكِيمِ
versets.tr: tanzīlu l-kitābi mina llāhi l-ʿazīzi l-ḥakīmi
versets.trad: The sending down of the Book is from God, the Mighty, the Wise.
versets.commentaire: The descent of the Book issues from the All-Mighty and the Wise. Qushayrī meditates on these two Names: He is the Mighty (al-ʿAzīz) in His majesty and His beginningless eternity, and the Wise (al-Ḥakīm) in His acts and in the gentleness with which He envelops the servant when He turns toward him. Might and wisdom do not oppose: the first inspires awe, the second reveals the hidden tenderness of the decree.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / ك ت ب
أ ل ه
ع ز ز / ح ك م

versets.ar: إِنَّ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ لَآيَاتٍ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: inna fī s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi la-āyātin li-l-muʾminīna
versets.trad: Surely in the heavens and the earth there are signs for the believers.
versets.commentaire: The heavens and the earth are laden with signs for the one who knows how to see. Qushayrī observes that the witnesses of Lordship shine forth and the proofs of divinity are manifest: whoever sobers from the drunkenness of heedlessness and lays his secret in the places of contemplation will unfailingly attain the realities of union. The sign lies not in the object alone, but in the unsealed eye that contemplates it.

mots.racine: س م و / أ ر ض
mots.sens: in the heavens and the earth

mots.racine: أ ي ي
أ م ن

versets.ar: وَفِي خَلْقِكُمْ وَمَا يَبُثُّ مِنْ دَابَّةٍ آيَاتٌ لِقَوْمٍ يُوقِنُونَ
versets.tr: wa-fī khalqikum wa-mā yabuthu min dābbatin āyātun li-qawmin yūqinūna
versets.trad: And in your own creation, and in the creatures He scatters abroad, there are signs for a people of certainty.
versets.commentaire: Your own constitution and the beasts spread across the earth are signs for the people of certainty. Qushayrī invites the servant to contemplate the uprightness of his stature, the fullness of his intellect, the gift of discernment, then to compare what he possesses with the other creatures: he will recognize that God has honored the children of Adam with understanding, knowledge and faith, and raised them above many of His creatures. Certainty is born of this comparing gaze that ascends from the gift to the Giver.

mots.racine: خ ل ق
ب ث ث / د ب ب
ي ق ن

versets.ar: وَاخْتِلَافِ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهَارِ وَمَا أَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مِنْ رِزْقٍ فَأَحْيَا بِهِ الْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهَا وَتَصْرِيفِ الرِّيَاحِ آيَاتٌ لِقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-khtilāfi l-layli wa-n-nahāri wa-mā anzala llāhu mina s-samāʾi min rizqin fa-aḥyā bihi l-arḍa baʿda mawtihā wa-taṣrīfi r-riyāḥi āyātun li-qawmin yaʿqilūna
versets.trad: And in the alternation of night and day, and the sustenance God sends down from the sky by which He revives the earth after its death, and in the shifting of the winds, there are signs for a people who reason.
versets.commentaire: The succession of night and day, the rain that revives the dead earth, the turning of the winds: so many signs for those who reason. Qushayrī teaches that God willed the sciences of religion to be acquired, validated by proofs and confirmed by witnesses: whoever is not illumined by them sees his foot slip from the straight path, plunged here below into the darkness of perplexity and blind imitation. To reason is to read in the world the signature of the One who moves it.

mots.racine: خ ل ف / ل ي ل / ن ه ر
mots.sens: the alternation of night and day

mots.racine: ر ز ق
ح ي ي / أ ر ض
mots.sens: He revives the earth by it

mots.racine: ص ر ف / ر و ح
ع ق ل

versets.ar: تِلْكَ آيَاتُ اللَّهِ نَتْلُوهَا عَلَيْكَ بِالْحَقِّ فَبِأَيِّ حَدِيثٍ بَعْدَ اللَّهِ وَآيَاتِهِ يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: tilka āyātu llāhi natlūhā ʿalayka bi-l-ḥaqqi fa-bi-ayyi ḥadīthin baʿda llāhi wa-āyātihi yuʾminūna
versets.trad: These are the signs of God which We recite to you in truth. In what discourse, after God and His signs, will they believe?
versets.commentaire: These are the signs of God, recited in all truth. Qushayrī lets the question rise like a sigh: the one who does not believe in them, in what discourse will he then believe? From what source will he draw after this? At what sea of Reality will he go to drink? Alas — no room remains for the slightest doubt. When God's Word falls silent at the hardened ear, nothing more can awaken it.

mots.racine: أ ي ي / أ ل ه
mots.sens: these are the signs of God

mots.racine: ت ل و
ح ق ق
ح د ث / أ م ن
mots.sens: in what discourse will they believe

versets.ar: وَيْلٌ لِكُلِّ أَفَّاكٍ أَثِيمٍ
versets.tr: waylun li-kulli affākin athīmin
versets.trad: Woe to every sinful liar:
versets.commentaire: Woe to the inveterate liar, steeped in sin! Qushayrī recalls that everything, even the mute, speaks: it falls silent in the language of words yet proclaims, by the proof it bears, the Oneness of its Author. The misery of the affāk is to remain deaf to this universal speech, to lie against the evidence that surrounds him on every side.

mots.racine: و ي ل
أ ف ك
أ ث م

versets.ar: يَسْمَعُ آيَاتِ اللَّهِ تُتْلَى عَلَيْهِ ثُمَّ يُصِرُّ مُسْتَكْبِرًا كَأَنْ لَمْ يَسْمَعْهَا فَبَشِّرْهُ بِعَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ
versets.tr: yasmaʿu āyāti llāhi tutlā ʿalayhi thumma yuṣirru mustakbiran ka-an lam yasmaʿhā fa-bashshirhu bi-ʿadhābin alīmin
versets.trad: He hears the signs of God recited to him, then persists in arrogance as if he had not heard them. So give him tidings of a painful punishment.
versets.commentaire: He hears the signs and yet persists, arrogant, as though he had heard nothing. Qushayrī contrasts two hearings: the one who lends the ear of understanding and looks by the light of Oneness wins the treasure of both abodes; the one who deafens himself through heedlessness falls into the hollow of ignorance and bears the searing brand of abandonment. The punishment is but deafness itself become destiny.

mots.racine: س م ع / أ ي ي
mots.sens: he hears the signs of God

mots.racine: ص ر ر / ك ب ر
س م ع
mots.sens: as if he had not heard them

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / أ ل م

versets.ar: وَإِذَا عَلِمَ مِنْ آيَاتِنَا شَيْئًا اتَّخَذَهَا هُزُوًا أُولَٰئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ مُهِينٌ
versets.tr: wa-idhā ʿalima min āyātinā shayʾan ittakhadhahā huzuwan ulāʾika lahum ʿadhābun muhīnun
versets.trad: And when he comes to know something of Our signs, he takes them in mockery. For such there is a humiliating punishment.
versets.commentaire: Learning something of Our signs, he makes of them only an object of ridicule. Qushayrī notes that he meets them with obstinacy, interpreting them by his own whim without grounding them in any sound chain. At times God discloses to the servant, in the depths of the heart, knowledge that no doubt can touch; but if he scorns it, he falls into the humiliation of the veil and the abasement of separation. The “humiliating” punishment answers the scorn exactly: whoever debases the signs will be debased.

mots.racine: ع ل م / أ ي ي
mots.sens: he learns something of Our signs

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / ه ز أ
ع ذ ب / ه و ن

versets.ar: مِنْ وَرَائِهِمْ جَهَنَّمُ وَلَا يُغْنِي عَنْهُمْ مَا كَسَبُوا شَيْئًا وَلَا مَا اتَّخَذُوا مِنْ دُونِ اللَّهِ أَوْلِيَاءَ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: min warāʾihim jahannamu wa-lā yughnī ʿanhum mā kasabū shayʾan wa-lā mā ittakhadhū min dūni llāhi awliyāʾa wa-lahum ʿadhābun ʿaẓīmun
versets.trad: Behind them lies Hell, and nothing they earned will avail them, nor the protectors they took besides God. For them awaits a mighty punishment.
versets.commentaire: Behind them is Hell, and nothing of their gains nor of their false protectors will avail them. Qushayrī evokes the hour of rupture: at that moment of trial no excuse is accepted from them, no plea is heard, and they are not restored to the unveiling of before. He seals the image with a piercing verse: now let the eye pour out its tears, for the days of limpid clarity return no more.

mots.racine: و ر ي / ج ه ن م
غ ن ي / ك س ب
د و ن / و ل ي
ع ذ ب / ع ظ م

versets.ar: هَٰذَا هُدًى وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِآيَاتِ رَبِّهِمْ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ مِنْ رِجْزٍ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: hādhā hudan wa-lladhīna kafarū bi-āyāti rabbihim lahum ʿadhābun min rijzin alīmun
versets.trad: This is guidance; and those who disbelieve in the signs of their Lord will have a punishment of painful defilement.
versets.commentaire: This is guidance; whoever denies the signs of his Lord will suffer a torment of painful defilement. Qushayrī makes of the Qurʾān a direction offered to all: the very Book that illumines the open heart becomes punishment for the one who turns away. Guidance is not neutral — it raises the one who receives it and confounds the one who refuses it.

mots.racine: ه د ي
ك ف ر / أ ي ي
mots.sens: they deny the signs of their Lord

mots.racine: ع ذ ب / ر ج ز / أ ل م

versets.ar: اللَّهُ الَّذِي سَخَّرَ لَكُمُ الْبَحْرَ لِتَجْرِيَ الْفُلْكُ فِيهِ بِأَمْرِهِ وَلِتَبْتَغُوا مِنْ فَضْلِهِ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: allāhu lladhī sakhkhara lakumu l-baḥra li-tajriya l-fulku fīhi bi-amrihi wa-li-tabtaghū min faḍlihi wa-laʿallakum tashkurūna
versets.trad: It is God who subjected the sea to you, that ships may sail upon it by His command, that you may seek of His bounty, and that you may give thanks.
versets.commentaire: God has subjected the sea so that ships may sail upon it by His command. Qushayrī reads in it a parable of the soul: the servant is in the vessel of clinging to God upon the seas of the decree; the winds of solicitude drive him, the sails of trust are raised, and the paths are clear upon the sea of certainty. As long as the winds of safety blow, the ship is saved; but should the gusts of trial arise, nothing remains in the sailor's hands — the decrees prevail, and soon the hearts rise to the throats.

mots.racine: س خ ر / ب ح ر
mots.sens: He subjected the sea to you

mots.racine: ج ر ي / ف ل ك
ب غ ي / ف ض ل
ش ك ر

versets.ar: وَسَخَّرَ لَكُمْ مَا فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَمَا فِي الْأَرْضِ جَمِيعًا مِنْهُ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَآيَاتٍ لِقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-sakhkhara lakum mā fī s-samāwāti wa-mā fī l-arḍi jamīʿan minhu inna fī dhālika la-āyātin li-qawmin yatafakkarūna
versets.trad: And He subjected to you all that is in the heavens and the earth, all of it from Him. In that are signs for a people who reflect.
versets.commentaire: All that is in the heavens and the earth is subjected to you, and all of it is from Him. Qushayrī draws from “all of it from Him” a lesson in freedom: there is nothing from which man does not derive benefit, and all of it proceeds from Him — the sky for a roof, the earth for a cradle. What deception, then, to let yourself be enslaved by what is enslaved to you! Let the servant reflect: were a single piece of the world's order to fail — the sun, the night, the moon — what would become of his life?

mots.racine: س خ ر / س م و
mots.sens: He subjected to you what is in the heavens

mots.racine: ج م ع
ف ك ر

versets.ar: قُلْ لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا يَغْفِرُوا لِلَّذِينَ لَا يَرْجُونَ أَيَّامَ اللَّهِ لِيَجْزِيَ قَوْمًا بِمَا كَانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ
versets.tr: qul li-lladhīna āmanū yaghfirū li-lladhīna lā yarjūna ayyāma llāhi li-yajziya qawman bi-mā kānū yaksibūna
versets.trad: Tell those who believe to forgive those who do not hope for the days of God, that He may requite a people for what they used to earn.
versets.commentaire: Tell the believers to forgive those who do not hope for the days of God. Qushayrī sees in this verse a summons to the noblest character: to fine companionship, to overlooking the ignorance of others, to purification from the turbidities of human nature and the demands of avarice. And he reassures: none escapes God; whoever wishes to know how He guards His friends and destroys His enemies need only wait a few days to see where the paths lead.

mots.racine: ق و ل / أ م ن
غ ف ر
ر ج و / ي و م
mots.sens: do not hope for the days of God

mots.racine: ج ز ي
mots.sens: that He may requite a people

versets.ar: مَنْ عَمِلَ صَالِحًا فَلِنَفْسِهِ وَمَنْ أَسَاءَ فَعَلَيْهَا ثُمَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكُمْ تُرْجَعُونَ
versets.tr: man ʿamila ṣāliḥan fa-li-nafsihi wa-man asāʾa fa-ʿalayhā thumma ilā rabbikum turjaʿūna
versets.trad: Whoever does good does so for himself, and whoever does evil does so against himself; then to your Lord you will be returned.
versets.commentaire: Whoever acts well works for himself, whoever acts ill works against himself, and then all return to their Lord. Qushayrī sums it up with luminous concision: to the one who does good belongs his bliss, to the one who commits evil his torment; then the return is to the Master. The act is not lost in the void: it makes its return, like the soul itself, to the One from whom it issued.

mots.racine: ع م ل / ص ل ح
ن ف س
س و أ
ر ب ب / ر ج ع
mots.sens: you will be returned to your Lord

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنَا بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ الْكِتَابَ وَالْحُكْمَ وَالنُّبُوَّةَ وَرَزَقْنَاهُمْ مِنَ الطَّيِّبَاتِ وَفَضَّلْنَاهُمْ عَلَى الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-laqad ātaynā banī isrāʾīla l-kitāba wa-l-ḥukma wa-n-nubuwwata wa-razaqnāhum mina ṭ-ṭayyibāti wa-faḍḍalnāhum ʿalā l-ʿālamīna
versets.trad: We gave the Children of Israel the Book, judgment and prophethood; We provided them with good things and favored them above the worlds.
themes: the gifts to the Children of Israel
versets.commentaire: To the Children of Israel We gave the Book, wisdom, prophethood, good provisions and a rank of election. Qushayrī observes that God repeatedly recalls Moses and the Children of Israel, now in summary, now in detail; here He sums up before returning to the mention of our Prophet. The evocation of the prophetic past prepares the announcement of the new way entrusted to Muḥammad.

mots.racine: أ ت ي
mots.sens: We gave the Children of Israel

mots.racine: ك ت ب / ح ك م / ن ب أ
ف ض ل / ع ل م
mots.sens: We favored them above the worlds

versets.ar: وَآتَيْنَاهُمْ بَيِّنَاتٍ مِنَ الْأَمْرِ فَمَا اخْتَلَفُوا إِلَّا مِنْ بَعْدِ مَا جَاءَهُمُ الْعِلْمُ بَغْيًا بَيْنَهُمْ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ يَقْضِي بَيْنَهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ فِيمَا كَانُوا فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ātaynāhum bayyinātin mina l-amri fa-mā ikhtalafū illā min baʿdi mā jāʾahumu l-ʿilmu baghyan baynahum inna rabbaka yaqḍī baynahum yawma l-qiyāmati fīmā kānū fīhi yakhtalifūna
versets.trad: We gave them clear proofs of the matter; and they did not differ until after knowledge had come to them, out of mutual envy. Surely your Lord will judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that wherein they differed.
versets.commentaire: They differed only after knowledge had come to them, out of mutual envy. Qushayrī underscores the bitter paradox: it was not ignorance but knowledge itself, twisted by covetousness, that divided them. Knowledge without humility becomes the seed of discord; and the Lord reserves to Himself the judgment, on the Day of Resurrection, of what men have entangled.

mots.racine: ب ي ن / أ م ر

mots.ar: مَا اخْتَلَفُوا إِلَّا مِنْ بَعْدِ الْعِلْمِ
mots.tr: mā ikhtalafū illā min baʿdi l-ʿilmi
mots.racine: خ ل ف / ع ل م
ب غ ي
ق ض ي

versets.ar: ثُمَّ جَعَلْنَاكَ عَلَىٰ شَرِيعَةٍ مِنَ الْأَمْرِ فَاتَّبِعْهَا وَلَا تَتَّبِعْ أَهْوَاءَ الَّذِينَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: thumma jaʿalnāka ʿalā sharīʿatin mina l-amri fa-ttabiʿhā wa-lā tattabiʿ ahwāʾa lladhīna lā yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: Then We placed you upon a clear way (sharīʿa) of the Command; so follow it, and do not follow the desires of those who do not know.
versets.commentaire: We set you upon a way issuing from the Command: follow it, and do not follow the whims of the ignorant. Qushayrī hears God say to the Prophet: We have singled you out with subtleties, so know them; We have traced for you paths, so walk them; We have established for you realities, so do not overstep them nor incline to follow another than Us. The sharīʿa is not constraint but privilege: a road cut for the chosen one.

mots.racine: ج ع ل / ش ر ع
mots.sens: We placed you upon a way

mots.racine: أ م ر
ت ب ع
ه و ي / ع ل م

versets.ar: إِنَّهُمْ لَنْ يُغْنُوا عَنْكَ مِنَ اللَّهِ شَيْئًا وَإِنَّ الظَّالِمِينَ بَعْضُهُمْ أَوْلِيَاءُ بَعْضٍ وَاللَّهُ وَلِيُّ الْمُتَّقِينَ
versets.tr: innahum lan yughnū ʿanka mina llāhi shayʾan wa-inna ẓ-ẓālimīna baʿḍuhum awliyāʾu baʿḍin wa-llāhu waliyyu l-muttaqīna
versets.trad: They will avail you nothing against God. The wrongdoers are allies of one another, but God is the Protector of the godfearing.
versets.commentaire: These will avail you nothing against God; God is the ally of the godfearing. Qushayrī draws from it a counsel of surrender: if He wills you a grace, none withholds it; if He wills you a trial, none averts it. So attach your thought to no creature, turn your inmost being toward nothing: trust your Lord and rely upon Him. True protection is found only with the one Protector.

mots.racine: غ ن ي
ظ ل م / و ل ي
mots.sens: the wrongdoers are allies of one another

mots.racine: و ل ي / و ق ي
mots.sens: God is the ally of the godfearing

versets.ar: هَٰذَا بَصَائِرُ لِلنَّاسِ وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةٌ لِقَوْمٍ يُوقِنُونَ
versets.tr: hādhā baṣāʾiru li-n-nāsi wa-hudan wa-raḥmatun li-qawmin yūqinūna
versets.trad: This is enlightenment for mankind, and guidance and mercy for a people of certainty.
versets.commentaire: These are lights of inner sight for mankind, guidance and mercy for the people of certainty. Qushayrī describes the degrees of vision: there is the one who sees by the light of reason, the one who penetrates by perspicacity (firāsa), the one who looks by the certainty of a proof, the one who sees by the eye of faith, and the one who contemplates by the light of inner sight — that one is in full day, his sun risen, his sky cloudless. The baṣīra is the inner dawn where proof gives way to vision.

mots.racine: ب ص ر
ه د ي / ر ح م
ي ق ن

versets.ar: أَمْ حَسِبَ الَّذِينَ اجْتَرَحُوا السَّيِّئَاتِ أَنْ نَجْعَلَهُمْ كَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ سَوَاءً مَحْيَاهُمْ وَمَمَاتُهُمْ سَاءَ مَا يَحْكُمُونَ
versets.tr: am ḥasiba lladhīna jtaraḥū s-sayyiʾāti an najʿalahum ka-lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti sawāʾan maḥyāhum wa-mamātuhum sāʾa mā yaḥkumūna
versets.trad: Do those who commit evils think We shall make them like those who believe and do righteous deeds, equal in their life and their death? Evil is the judgment they make!
versets.commentaire: Do the evildoers imagine they will be treated like the righteous believers, equal in life and death? Qushayrī sends forth a cascade of burning questions: is the one We have lowered to the depths of baseness like the one We have raised to the heights of grace? Is the one whose hand We took and whom We wrapped in mercy like the one trampled by abandonment? Is the one We drew near and made Our own like the one whose heart We did not revive?

mots.racine: ج ر ح / س و أ
أ م ن / ع م ل
س و ي / ح ي ي / م و ت
mots.sens: equal in their life and death

mots.racine: س و أ / ح ك م

versets.ar: وَخَلَقَ اللَّهُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ بِالْحَقِّ وَلِتُجْزَىٰ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ بِمَا كَسَبَتْ وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-khalaqa llāhu s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa bi-l-ḥaqqi wa-li-tujzā kullu nafsin bi-mā kasabat wa-hum lā yuẓlamūna
versets.trad: God created the heavens and the earth in truth, and that every soul may be requited for what it has earned, and they will not be wronged.
versets.commentaire: God created the heavens and the earth in truth, that every soul might be requited without injustice. Qushayrī recalls that creation is no idle play: it is strained toward justice, where each soul receives the exact measure of what it has earned. The Truth (al-ḥaqq) that founds the world is the same that will settle its outcome: not a grain of good or evil will be wronged in it.

mots.racine: خ ل ق / ح ق ق
mots.sens: He created heavens and earth in truth

mots.racine: ج ز ي / ن ف س
ظ ل م

versets.ar: أَفَرَأَيْتَ مَنِ اتَّخَذَ إِلَٰهَهُ هَوَاهُ وَأَضَلَّهُ اللَّهُ عَلَىٰ عِلْمٍ وَخَتَمَ عَلَىٰ سَمْعِهِ وَقَلْبِهِ وَجَعَلَ عَلَىٰ بَصَرِهِ غِشَاوَةً فَمَنْ يَهْدِيهِ مِنْ بَعْدِ اللَّهِ أَفَلَا تَذَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: a-fa-raʾayta mani ttakhadha ilāhahu hawāhu wa-aḍallahu llāhu ʿalā ʿilmin wa-khatama ʿalā samʿihi wa-qalbihi wa-jaʿala ʿalā baṣarihi ghishāwatan fa-man yahdīhi min baʿdi llāhi a-fa-lā tadhakkarūna
versets.trad: Have you seen the one who takes his own desire as his god, whom God leads astray knowingly, and seals his hearing and his heart, and places a covering over his sight? Who then will guide him after God? Will you not remember?
versets.commentaire: See the one who takes his desire for his god: God leads him astray knowingly, seals his hearing and his heart, and veils his sight. Qushayrī warns: the one who does not tread the way of following, does not fulfill the disciplines of ascesis, does not strip himself wholly of his whim and is not trained by an exemplary master — that one drifts into every pit and wanders in every misguidance. His loss exceeds his gain! They perform acts of nearness at the prompting of their restless soul — their bridle is in the hand of their passion; these are the people of cunning, drawn step by step to their ruin without sensing it.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / أ ل ه / ه و ي
mots.sens: he takes his desire as his god
mots.def: hawā: whim set up as idol.

mots.racine: ض ل ل / ع ل م
خ ت م / س م ع / ق ل ب
mots.sens: He seals his hearing and heart

mots.racine: غ ش و
ه د ي
mots.sens: who will guide him after God

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا مَا هِيَ إِلَّا حَيَاتُنَا الدُّنْيَا نَمُوتُ وَنَحْيَا وَمَا يُهْلِكُنَا إِلَّا الدَّهْرُ وَمَا لَهُمْ بِذَٰلِكَ مِنْ عِلْمٍ إِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا يَظُنُّونَ
versets.tr: wa-qālū mā hiya illā ḥayātunā d-dunyā namūtu wa-naḥyā wa-mā yuhlikunā illā d-dahru wa-mā lahum bi-dhālika min ʿilmin in hum illā yaẓunnūna
versets.trad: They say: “There is nothing but our life in this world; we die and we live, and nothing destroys us but time.” Yet they have no knowledge of that; they only conjecture.
versets.commentaire: They say there is no life but this world's, and that only time destroys them. Qushayrī paints them mercilessly: they learned nothing from the fate of those before them, they spent their lives in animality, they spared their hearts the labor of thought; they were enlightened by no science and drew upon no reality. Their capital is conjecture — and they remain in heedlessness.

mots.racine: ح ي ي / د ن و
mots.sens: there is only our worldly life

mots.racine: م و ت / ح ي ي
ه ل ك / د ه ر
ظ ن ن

versets.ar: وَإِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ آيَاتُنَا بَيِّنَاتٍ مَا كَانَ حُجَّتَهُمْ إِلَّا أَنْ قَالُوا ائْتُوا بِآبَائِنَا إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صَادِقِينَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā tutlā ʿalayhim āyātunā bayyinātin mā kāna ḥujjatahum illā an qālū ʾtū bi-ābāʾinā in kuntum ṣādiqīna
versets.trad: When Our clear signs are recited to them, their only argument is to say: “Bring back our fathers, if you are truthful!”
versets.commentaire: Recited with full clarity, Our signs draw from them only a challenge: “Bring back our fathers, if you speak the truth!” Qushayrī observes that they demand the resurrection of their dead as an impossible condition — but soon they will see, fulfilled, what they deemed improbable. Denial disguises itself as a demand for proof, while the proof already surrounds them.

mots.racine: ت ل و / أ ي ي / ب ي ن
mots.sens: Our clear signs are recited to them

mots.racine: ح ج ج
أ ت ي / أ ب و

versets.ar: قُلِ اللَّهُ يُحْيِيكُمْ ثُمَّ يُمِيتُكُمْ ثُمَّ يَجْمَعُكُمْ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: quli llāhu yuḥyīkum thumma yumītukum thumma yajmaʿukum ilā yawmi l-qiyāmati lā rayba fīhi wa-lākinna akthara n-nāsi lā yaʿlamūna
versets.trad: Say: “God gives you life, then causes you to die, then gathers you for the Day of Resurrection, of which there is no doubt; but most people do not know.”
versets.commentaire: Say: God gives you life, makes you die, then gathers you for the indubitable Day. Qushayrī answers their challenge: they demanded the resurrection of their dead; let them know that the One who gives life, takes it back, and gathers is alone the master of the Hour. The whole cycle — birth, death, reunion — is but the expression of a single Sovereignty that most ignore.

mots.racine: ح ي ي
م و ت
ج م ع / ق و م
mots.sens: He gathers you for the Day

mots.racine: ر ي ب

versets.ar: وَلِلَّهِ مُلْكُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَيَوْمَ تَقُومُ السَّاعَةُ يَوْمَئِذٍ يَخْسَرُ الْمُبْطِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-li-llāhi mulku s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-yawma taqūmu s-sāʿatu yawmaʾidhin yakhsaru l-mubṭilūna
versets.trad: To God belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth. On the Day the Hour arrives, on that Day the falsifiers will be lost.
versets.commentaire: To God belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth; on the Day the Hour arises, the champions of falsehood will be lost. Qushayrī announces that when God raises up the Resurrection, He will gather the people of vanity, and the day of dispute will come upon them. The Kingdom that lay veiled beneath the world's appearance will be wholly disclosed — and those who clung to nothingness will have nothing left to hold.

mots.racine: م ل ك / س م و / أ ر ض
mots.sens: to God the dominion of heavens and earth

mots.racine: ق و م / س و ع
خ س ر / ب ط ل

versets.ar: وَتَرَىٰ كُلَّ أُمَّةٍ جَاثِيَةً كُلُّ أُمَّةٍ تُدْعَىٰ إِلَىٰ كِتَابِهَا الْيَوْمَ تُجْزَوْنَ مَا كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-tarā kulla ummatin jāthiyatan kullu ummatin tudʿā ilā kitābihā l-yawma tujzawna mā kuntum taʿmalūna
versets.trad: You will see every community kneeling. Every community will be summoned to its Book: “Today you are requited for what you used to do.”
versets.commentaire: You will see every community kneeling (jāthiya), summoned to its Book. Qushayrī makes of this universal kneeling the confession of nothingness before Sovereignty: each one, reduced to powerlessness, awaits the verdict of its register. The word jāthiya — from which the sūra takes its name — depicts humanity bowed upon its knees, hanging upon the reading of its own deeds, with no refuge left but the truth of its book.

mots.racine: ر أ ي / أ م م / ج ث و
د ع و / ك ت ب
ج ز ي

versets.ar: هَٰذَا كِتَابُنَا يَنْطِقُ عَلَيْكُمْ بِالْحَقِّ إِنَّا كُنَّا نَسْتَنْسِخُ مَا كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: hādhā kitābunā yanṭiqu ʿalaykum bi-l-ḥaqqi innā kunnā nastansikhu mā kuntum taʿmalūna
versets.trad: This is Our Book; it speaks against you in truth. We were recording all that you used to do.
versets.commentaire: This is Our Book, testifying against you in truth: We were recording all your deeds. Qushayrī recalls that nothing escapes the divine inscription — every act, secret or public, was transcribed in its time. The Book “that speaks” is God's infallible memory made visible: it accuses not from cruelty but from exactness, returning to each the precise trace of his life.

mots.racine: ك ت ب
ن ط ق / ح ق ق
mots.sens: it speaks against you in truth

mots.racine: ن س خ / ع م ل

versets.ar: فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ فَيُدْخِلُهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ فِي رَحْمَتِهِ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ الْفَوْزُ الْمُبِينُ
versets.tr: fa-ammā lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti fa-yudkhiluhum rabbuhum fī raḥmatihi dhālika huwa l-fawzu l-mubīnu
versets.trad: As for those who believed and did righteous deeds, their Lord will admit them into His mercy. That is the manifest triumph.
versets.commentaire: The righteous believers, their Lord admits them into His mercy: that is the manifest triumph. Qushayrī sums up the Day's division: the believers have triumphed and prevailed, while the deniers have perished and vanished. The “manifest triumph” is not merely the Garden, but the very entrance into Mercy — to be welcomed within the tenderness of the Lord.

mots.racine: أ م ن / ع م ل / ص ل ح
mots.sens: those who believed and did good

mots.racine: د خ ل / ر ب ب / ر ح م
mots.sens: their Lord admits them into His mercy

mots.racine: ف و ز / ب ي ن

versets.ar: وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَفَلَمْ تَكُنْ آيَاتِي تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْكُمْ فَاسْتَكْبَرْتُمْ وَكُنْتُمْ قَوْمًا مُجْرِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-ammā lladhīna kafarū a-fa-lam takun āyātī tutlā ʿalaykum fa-stakbartum wa-kuntum qawman mujrimīna
versets.trad: But as for those who disbelieved: “Were My signs not recited to you? Yet you were arrogant and became a guilty people.”
versets.commentaire: To the deniers it will be said: Were My signs not recited to you? Yet you grew arrogant. Qushayrī hears the accusing question of the Day: are you not those who, when told of your final end, called your own Master a liar? Arrogance was the bolt: they heard and refused, and their crime was less ignorance than the haughty scorn of the Word.

mots.racine: أ ي ي / ت ل و
mots.sens: were My signs not recited to you

mots.racine: ك ب ر
ج ر م

versets.ar: وَإِذَا قِيلَ إِنَّ وَعْدَ اللَّهِ حَقٌّ وَالسَّاعَةُ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهَا قُلْتُمْ مَا نَدْرِي مَا السَّاعَةُ إِنْ نَظُنُّ إِلَّا ظَنًّا وَمَا نَحْنُ بِمُسْتَيْقِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā qīla inna waʿda llāhi ḥaqqun wa-s-sāʿatu lā rayba fīhā qultum mā nadrī mā s-sāʿatu in naẓunnu illā ẓannan wa-mā naḥnu bi-mustayqinīna
versets.trad: When it was said, “The promise of God is true, and the Hour, there is no doubt of it,” you said, “We do not know what the Hour is; we only suppose, and we are not convinced.”
versets.commentaire: At the announcement that God's promise is true and the Hour beyond doubt, they answered with suspicion. Qushayrī ranks this refusal among the fruits of the conjecture that was their whole capital: they were enlightened by no science and grounded in no reality. Their “we are not convinced” is the confession of a heart that has chosen doubt as its dwelling, shutting the door on the certainty that knocked.

mots.racine: و ع د / ح ق ق
د ر ي / س و ع
mots.sens: we do not know what the Hour is

mots.racine: ظ ن ن
ي ق ن

versets.ar: وَبَدَا لَهُمْ سَيِّئَاتُ مَا عَمِلُوا وَحَاقَ بِهِمْ مَا كَانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِئُونَ
versets.tr: wa-badā lahum sayyiʾātu mā ʿamilū wa-ḥāqa bihim mā kānū bihi yastahziʾūna
versets.trad: The evils of what they did will appear to them, and what they used to mock will engulf them.
versets.commentaire: Their evil deeds will appear to them, and what they mocked will close in upon them. Qushayrī recalls that mockery returns to enclose the mocker: what they turned to ridicule now hems them in on every side. On the Day of unveiling, the hidden act becomes a face, and the old derision becomes the punishment that encircles them.

mots.racine: ب د و / س و أ / ع م ل
mots.sens: their evil deeds appear to them

mots.racine: ح ي ق
ه ز أ

versets.ar: وَقِيلَ الْيَوْمَ نَنْسَاكُمْ كَمَا نَسِيتُمْ لِقَاءَ يَوْمِكُمْ هَٰذَا وَمَأْوَاكُمُ النَّارُ وَمَا لَكُمْ مِنْ نَاصِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qīla l-yawma nansākum kamā nasītum liqāʾa yawmikum hādhā wa-maʾwākumu n-nāru wa-mā lakum min nāṣirīna
versets.trad: It will be said: “Today We forget you as you forgot the meeting of this Day of yours. Your dwelling is the Fire, and you have no helpers.”
versets.commentaire: It will be said: Today We forget you as you forgot the meeting of this Day. Qushayrī makes the sentence of spiritual retribution resound: today — since you forgot Us — We forget you, and the Fire is your dwelling. The forgetting is no failing in God but the exact mirror of their own negligence: they turned the heart from the Meeting, and the Meeting turns from them.

mots.racine: ن س ي
ن س ي / ل ق ي
mots.sens: as you forgot the meeting of your Day

mots.racine: أ و ي / ن و ر
ن ص ر

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكُمْ بِأَنَّكُمُ اتَّخَذْتُمْ آيَاتِ اللَّهِ هُزُوًا وَغَرَّتْكُمُ الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا فَالْيَوْمَ لَا يُخْرَجُونَ مِنْهَا وَلَا هُمْ يُسْتَعْتَبُونَ
versets.tr: dhālikum bi-annakumu ttakhadhtum āyāti llāhi huzuwan wa-gharratkumu l-ḥayātu d-dunyā fa-l-yawma lā yukhrajūna minhā wa-lā hum yustaʿtabūna
versets.trad: That is because you took the signs of God in mockery, and the life of this world deluded you. So today they will not be brought out of it, nor will they be allowed to make amends.
versets.commentaire: That is because you mocked the signs of God and the life of this world deluded you. Qushayrī shows the twofold fault: the derision of the signs and the illusion of the world, two veils that seal forever the door of return. On the Day when repentance is no longer accepted, the delusion reveals itself for what it was: a fascination that cost eternity.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / ه ز أ
mots.sens: you mocked the signs of God

mots.racine: غ ر ر / ح ي ي
خ ر ج
mots.sens: they will not be brought out

mots.racine: ع ت ب

versets.ar: فَلِلَّهِ الْحَمْدُ رَبِّ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَرَبِّ الْأَرْضِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: fa-li-llāhi l-ḥamdu rabbi s-samāwāti wa-rabbi l-arḍi rabbi l-ʿālamīna
versets.trad: So to God belongs all praise, Lord of the heavens and Lord of the earth, Lord of the worlds!
versets.commentaire: Praise belongs to God, Lord of the heavens, of the earth and of the worlds. Qushayrī unfolds the thanksgiving: praise to Him for what He brings to light and creates, for what He vivifies and annihilates, for what He sets in motion and lets pass — for to Him belongs the decree, to Him the majesty, to Him the grandeur and the splendor. The sūra that denounced the deniers' ingratitude ends on the pure outpouring of gratitude.

mots.racine: ح م د
ر ب ب / س م و / أ ر ض
mots.sens: Lord of the heavens and the earth

mots.racine: ر ب ب / ع ل م

versets.ar: وَلَهُ الْكِبْرِيَاءُ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الْحَكِيمُ
versets.tr: wa-lahu l-kibriyāʾu fī s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-huwa l-ʿazīzu l-ḥakīmu
versets.trad: To Him belongs all grandeur in the heavens and the earth; and He is the Mighty, the Wise.
versets.commentaire: To Him belongs grandeur in the heavens and the earth; He is the Mighty, the Wise. Qushayrī seals the sūra upon absolute Lordship: to Him the judgment, to Him the rightful pride of Grandeur (kibriyāʾ), to Him the majesty and the loftiness. The two Names that opened the revelation at verse 2 — al-ʿAzīz, al-Ḥakīm — close it again, like a perfect circle in which Might and Wisdom envelop the whole arc of the sūra.

mots.racine: ك ب ر
س م و / أ ر ض
mots.sens: in the heavens and the earth

mots.racine: ع ز ز / ح ك م
mots.sens: He is the Mighty, the Wise
